rulings.Case Title: JASON DEWITT VS. JACINTO JOSE VALDOVINO ET AL **Provide Access**
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE Amended ANSWER to CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the LAW AND MOTION / DISCOVERY Calendar for Friday, May-08-2026, LINE 1. CROSS COMPLAINANT JACINTO VALDOVINO'S #AM3654 **PROVIDE ACCESS** MOTION TO STRIKE Amended ANSWER to CROSS COMPLAINT. Defendant and cross-complainant Jacinto Jose Valdovino's motion is denied. Valdovino seeks miscellaneous relief, including an opportunity to demur to an answer to a supplemental cross-complaint that he contends was never served on him. The court denies this relief; there is a proof of service attached to the cross-complaint and the court credits it. The court denies Valdovino's request for an order specifying any particular kind of service. The Code of Civil Procedure sets out permissible means of service and the court lacks authority to compel parties to use one kind of permitted service rather than another. The court denies Valdovino's request for an order clarifying and confirming a prior order; the request is an untimely request for reconsideration under Code of Civil Procedure, section 1008, subdivision (a). For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: THE CIERNY FIRM VS. SLOT KICKS, LLC, A NEW YORK LIMITED LIABILITY
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the LAW AND MOTION / DISCOVERY Calendar for Friday, May-08-2026, LINE 4. DEFENDANT CHRISTOPHER KAPRAT's, AN INDIVIDUAL, MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND. Defendant Chris Kaprat's motion to set aside default is continued for hearing to June 5, 2026. Kaprat shall file an amended declaration attaching his proposed responsive pleading by no later than May 22, 2026. Plaintiff may file a sur-reply by May 29, 2026. Code of Civil Procedure, Sec. 473, subdivision (b), provides in relevant part that "[t]he court may, upon any terms as may be just, relieve a party or the party's legal representative from a judgment, dismissal, order, or other proceeding taken against the party through the party's mistake, inadvertence, surprise, or excusable neglect. Application for this relief shall be accompanied by a copy of the answer or other pleading proposed to be filed therein, otherwise the application shall not be granted . . . ." Kaprat has not attached a proposed answer or other pleading, a mandatory requirement of the statute. In the interests of justice, the court continues the motion to permit him to remedy this defect in his moving papers. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely.
